name: Hotel Welco Narita ( Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita )
address: 818-1, Hanazaki-cho, Sân Bay Quốc Tế Narita, Narita, Nhật Bản
description:
Khách sạn Welco Narita (trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita), Narita, Nhật Bản

Khách sạn Welco Narita (trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita) nằm ở Narita, Nhật Bản, là một khách sạn 3.0 sao đáng chú ý. Với vị trí thuận lợi, khách sạn chỉ cách sân bay 15 phút đi xe. Với 247 phòng, khách sạn này cung cấp một trải nghiệm lưu trú thoải mái cho du khách. Nằm cách trung tâm thành phố chỉ 0.1 km, khách sạn cũng thu hút du khách bởi sự tiện lợi trong việc khám phá các điểm tham quan và mua sắm. 

Thời gian nhận phòng là từ 02:00 PM, và thời gian trả phòng là cho đến 11:00 AM. Đối với chính sách trẻ em, khách sạn này không cho phép trẻ em ở miễn phí. Có thể có các khoản phụ phí áp dụng cho trẻ em.

Tiện nghi giải trí tại Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita)

Với một loạt các tiện ích giải trí, Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) là lựa chọn lý tưởng cho những du khách muốn thư giãn và tận hưởng kỳ nghỉ tuyệt vời tại Narita, Nhật Bản. Khách sạn này có một quầy bar đẹp mắt, nơi du khách có thể thưởng thức những loại đồ uống phong phú và thức ăn ngon miệng trong một không gian thoải mái và ấm cúng. Ngoài ra, khách sạn còn có một salon chăm sóc tóc chuyên nghiệp, nơi du khách có thể thư giãn và làm mới bản thân sau một ngày dài khám phá thành phố. Với các tiện nghi giải trí này, Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) đảm bảo mang đến cho du khách những trải nghiệm thú vị và thoải mái.

Tiện nghi tiện lợi tại Khách sạn Welco Narita (Trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita)

Khách sạn Welco Narita (Trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita) tọa lạc tại Narita, Nhật Bản, mang đến cho du khách những tiện nghi tiện lợi đáng chú ý. Khách sạn cung cấp dịch vụ giặt ủi, hộp đựng đồ an toàn, Wi-Fi miễn phí trong khu vực công cộng và Wi-Fi miễn phí trong tất cả các phòng. Du khách có thể yên tâm với dịch vụ giặt khô, dịch vụ lưu trữ hành lý và máy bán hàng tự động. Bên cạnh đó, khách sạn còn cung cấp dịch vụ dọn phòng hàng ngày và tiện nghi laundromat để du khách có thể giặt giũ tiện lợi.

Tiện ích vận chuyển tại Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita)

Khi lựa chọn lưu trú tại Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita), quý khách sẽ được trải nghiệm những tiện ích vận chuyển tuyệt vời. Khách sạn cung cấp chỗ đỗ xe ô tô, giúp quý khách dễ dàng di chuyển trong khu vực. Ngoài ra, dịch vụ taxi cũng được cung cấp tại khách sạn, giúp quý khách di chuyển thuận tiện và nhanh chóng. 

Khách sạn Welco Narita còn có chỗ đỗ xe ô tô trên địa điểm, giúp quý khách tiết kiệm thời gian và công sức khi tìm kiếm chỗ đỗ xe. Đặc biệt, việc đỗ xe tại khách sạn là miễn phí, giúp quý khách không phải lo lắng về chi phí phát sinh. Với những tiện ích vận chuyển này, Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) sẽ mang đến cho quý khách một trải nghiệm lưu trú tiện lợi và thoải mái.

Tiện nghi ẩm thực tại Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita)

Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) là một khách sạn tuyệt vời với nhiều tiện nghi ẩm thực đa dạng. Khách sạn có một nhà hàng đẳng cấp, nơi bạn có thể thưởng thức những món ăn ngon và đa dạng từ khắp nơi trên thế giới. Bữa sáng tại đây cũng rất đáng chú ý với một bữa buffet phong phú, bao gồm cả bữa sáng kiểu châu Âu. Bạn có thể tận hưởng những món ăn ngon miệng và đầy năng lượng trước khi bắt đầu một ngày mới khám phá Narita.

Các loại phòng tại Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita)

Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) tọa lạc tại Narita, Nhật Bản và cung cấp các loại phòng đa dạng để đáp ứng nhu cầu của khách hàng. Khách sạn có phòng Standard Double Non-Smoking với diện tích 22 mét vuông và 1 giường đôi, phòng Standard Single Non-Smoking với diện tích 17 mét vuông và 1 giường đơn, cũng như phòng Standard Twin Non-Smoking với diện tích 25 mét vuông và 2 giường đơn. Đây là những lựa chọn lý tưởng cho du khách muốn tận hưởng không gian thoải mái và tiện nghi.

Khám phá Sân Bay Quốc Tế Narita - Cửa ngõ đến thành phố Narita

Sân Bay Quốc Tế Narita là một trong những sân bay quan trọng nhất ở Nhật Bản và là cửa ngõ chính để đến thành phố Narita. Với vị trí thuận lợi chỉ cách trung tâm thành phố khoảng 60 km, sân bay này là điểm dừng chân lý tưởng cho du khách đến Narita hoặc đi tiếp đến Tokyo. 

Sân Bay Quốc Tế Narita được biết đến với kiến trúc hiện đại và tiện nghi vượt trội. Du khách sẽ được trải nghiệm các dịch vụ chất lượng, từ các quầy check-in tiện lợi cho đến cửa hàng mua sắm, nhà hàng và quầy bar sang trọng. Ngoài ra, sân bay cũng có các phòng chờ thoải mái và các dịch vụ vận chuyển đến thành phố Narita và Tokyo. 

Khám phá Sân Bay Quốc Tế Narita là một trải nghiệm thú vị cho du khách đến Narita. Với vị trí thuận lợi và các tiện ích tuyệt vời, sân bay này là điểm đến lý tưởng để bắt đầu hoặc kết thúc hành trình của bạn.

Đến Hotel Welco Narita từ sân bay gần Narita

Hotel Welco Narita (trước đây là Mercure Hotel Narita) nằm ở Sân Bay Quốc Tế Narita, chỉ cách sân bay chính Narita khoảng 10 phút lái xe. Có hai sân bay gần Narita là Sân bay quốc tế Narita và Sân bay quốc tế Haneda. Để đến Hotel Welco Narita từ sân bay Narita, bạn có thể sử dụng dịch vụ xe buýt hoặc taxi.

Nếu bạn muốn tiết kiệm chi phí, dịch vụ xe buýt là lựa chọn phổ biến. Có nhiều hãng xe buýt chạy từ sân bay Narita đến Hotel Welco Narita. Thời gian di chuyển khoảng 10-15 phút và giá vé rất hợp lý. Bạn có thể tìm hiểu thêm về các hãng xe buýt và lịch trình tại quầy thông tin sân bay hoặc trên trang web của sân bay Narita.

Ngoài ra, bạn cũng có thể sử dụng dịch vụ taxi để đến Hotel Welco Narita. Taxi là lựa chọn thuận tiện và nhanh chóng, đặc biệt nếu bạn có nhiều hành lý. Bạn có thể tìm thấy các dịch vụ taxi tại sân bay Narita hoặc đặt trước qua điện thoại di động. Thời gian di chuyển bằng taxi cũng khoảng 10 phút và phí dịch vụ sẽ tùy thuộc vào khoảng cách và thời gian chờ đợi.

Khám phá những điểm tham quan gần Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita)

Nằm ở vị trí thuận lợi, Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) là một điểm khởi đầu tuyệt vời để khám phá những điểm tham quan nổi tiếng gần đó. Một trong số đó là Naritasan Shinsho-ji Temple, ngôi chùa cổ xưa với kiến trúc độc đáo và không gian yên bình, nơi du khách có thể tìm hiểu về văn hóa và lịch sử địa phương. 

Nếu bạn muốn tận hưởng không gian xanh mát, Naritasan Park và Sakura no yama Park là những điểm đến tuyệt vời. Tại đây, bạn có thể dạo bước trong khu vườn rộng lớn, ngắm nhìn những cánh đồng hoa anh đào đẹp mắt và thư giãn trong không gian tự nhiên tươi mát. 

Ngoài ra, bạn cũng có thể tận hưởng cảnh quan tuyệt đẹp từ Observation Deck at Narita Airport Terminal 2 và Narita International Airport Terminal 1 5F Observation Deck. Hai nơi này cung cấp một góc nhìn toàn cảnh về sân bay Narita và vùng đất xung quanh. Với vị trí thuận lợi và những điểm tham quan độc đáo, Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) là lựa chọn lý tưởng cho những du khách muốn khám phá và trải nghiệm vùng đất Narita.

Vị trí thuận lợi với các trạm giao thông công cộng gần Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita)

Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) tọa lạc tại một vị trí lý tưởng, với nhiều trạm giao thông công cộng tiện lợi trong khu vực xung quanh. Khách sạn chỉ cách trạm Narita Express (NEX) và trạm Shimosa-Kozaki vài phút đi bộ, giúp du khách dễ dàng di chuyển đến và đi từ sân bay Narita và các điểm đến khác ở Nhật Bản. Ngoài ra, các trạm gần đó như Narita Yukawa, Horinouchi và Namegawa Railway Station cũng cung cấp các tuyến tàu điện ngầm và tàu hỏa thuận tiện cho việc khám phá khu vực.

Nhà hàng xung quanh khách sạn Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita)

Khách sạn Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) nằm gần nhiều nhà hàng nổi tiếng như Kawatoyo Narita, Sushi Kyotatsu Narita Airport Terminal 1 Post Security Zone, CASUAL RESORT DINING SERENA, Yonagura Shokudo, Keawjai Narita, Sushichoshimaru Naritaten, Teco Teco, Ramen Shop Nemoto, Unagi Shinkawa và Ohsama-no-Maguro Shokudo. Với sự đa dạng về ẩm thực, du khách có thể dễ dàng tìm thấy những món ăn ngon từ Nhật Bản và quốc tế ngay trong khu vực xung quanh khách sạn. Từ món sushi tươi ngon, mì ramen thơm ngon đến các món ăn truyền thống Nhật Bản, du khách sẽ có trải nghiệm ẩm thực tuyệt vời tại Hotel Welco Narita.

Khám phá các điểm mua sắm xung quanh khách sạn Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita)

Khách sạn Hotel Welco Narita (Formerly Mercure Hotel Narita) nằm gần nhiều điểm mua sắm phổ biến tại Narita. Du khách có thể ghé thăm Narita Yokan Information Centre để tìm hiểu về ẩm thực Nhật Bản và mua các loại đặc sản địa phương. Nếu bạn là người yêu thích ẩm thực, Kuramoto Chokubaiten Chomeisen là một cửa hàng lý tưởng để mua các loại gia vị và nguyên liệu làm bánh Nhật Bản. Đừng quên ghé qua Pickles shop Kawamura Saheji Shoten để khám phá các loại muối chua đặc sản của Nhật Bản. Mon Chou-chou là một cửa hàng bánh ngọt nổi tiếng tại Narita, nơi bạn có thể thưởng thức những chiếc bánh ngọt tuyệt vời. Nếu bạn là người yêu thích hạt dẻ, hãy ghé qua Peanuts no Kimuraya để mua các loại hạt dẻ tươi ngon. Fujikura Shoten là một cửa hàng sách cổ điển, nơi bạn có thể tìm thấy những cuốn sách hiếm và độc đáo. Sabonsama là một cửa hàng bánh mì nổi tiếng, nơi bạn có thể thưởng thức những chiếc bánh mì tươi ngon. Cuối cùng, hãy ghé qua cửa hàng Sake CYOUMEISEN để trải nghiệm và mua sắm các loại rượu sake truyền thống của Nhật Bản.

Giá phòng trung bình tại Hotel Welco Narita so với giá phòng trung bình tại thành phố Narita

Với mức giá trung bình chỉ $69 một đêm, Hotel Welco Narita (dựa trên tên gọi trước đây là Mercure Hotel Narita) là một lựa chọn tuyệt vời cho du khách muốn trải nghiệm một kỳ nghỉ thoải mái và tiết kiệm tại thành phố Narita. So với giá trung bình của một phòng khách sạn tại Narita là $77, Hotel Welco Narita mang đến cho khách hàng một giá cả hợp lý và dịch vụ chất lượng cao. Với các tiện nghi hiện đại, phòng ốc thoáng đãng và đội ngũ nhân viên thân thiện, khách sạn này đảm bảo sự thoải mái và tiện nghi cho mọi khách hàng.

Khách sạn Welco Narita (trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita) - Đánh giá tích cực từ khách hàng

Khách sạn Welco Narita (trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita) đã nhận được những đánh giá tích cực từ khách hàng về sự sạch sẽ và thoải mái của phòng cũng như sự thân thiện của nhân viên. Phòng rất sạch sẽ và thoải mái, nhân viên rất thân thiện. Khách sạn rất tiện lợi và sạch sẽ. Giường thoải mái, phòng rất sạch sẽ và nhân viên thân thiện. Phòng rộng lớn và sạch sẽ. Đây là một khách sạn tuyệt vời với nhân viên rất nhiệt tình và thân thiện, tất cả mọi người ở đây đều rất sẵn lòng phục vụ. Tôi rất khuyến nghị. Nhân viên rất thân thiện. Dịch vụ của họ rất tốt. Phòng rộng rãi, sạch sẽ và thoải mái. Vòi sen mạnh mẽ.

Khách sạn Welco Narita (trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita) - Đánh giá tích cực từ khách hàng

Khách sạn Welco Narita (trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita) đã nhận được đánh giá tích cực từ khách hàng với tổng điểm 8.1 trên 10. Khách hàng đánh giá cao sự đáng giá của khách sạn với điểm 7.9, cho thấy giá trị tuyệt vời mà khách sạn mang lại. Các tiện nghi tại khách sạn cũng được đánh giá khá cao với điểm 7.4, đảm bảo sự thoải mái và tiện nghi cho du khách.

Một yếu tố quan trọng mà khách hàng đánh giá cao là vị trí của khách sạn, với điểm số 9.0. Khách sạn Welco Narita nằm ở vị trí thuận lợi, chỉ cách sân bay Narita không xa và có dễ dàng tiếp cận với các điểm tham quan nổi tiếng. Với vị trí đắc địa như vậy, khách hàng có thể dễ dàng khám phá thành phố Narita và tận hưởng những trải nghiệm tuyệt vời.

Khách hàng cũng đánh giá cao hiệu suất của nhân viên tại khách sạn, với điểm số 8.2. Đội ngũ nhân viên tận tâm và chuyên nghiệp, luôn sẵn sàng hỗ trợ khách hàng và đáp ứng mọi yêu cầu. Với dịch vụ chất lượng và sự thân thiện của nhân viên, khách hàng có thể yên tâm và tận hưởng kỳ nghỉ tuyệt vời tại khách sạn Welco Narita (trước đây là Khách sạn Mercure Narita).

sample_comments.text:
Mức giá phòng rẻ hơn so với các khách sạn thương mại khác trong khu vực, nhưng kích thước phòng lại lớn hơn, bên trong khách sạn có nhiều nhà hàng và có thể đặt dịch vụ phòng, rất đầy đủ tiện nghi. Vị trí cũng gần ga tàu, có nhiều cửa hàng tiện lợi và đồ ăn nhanh, cùng với tiệm bánh mở cửa từ sớm, tất cả đều trong khoảng cách đi bộ rất thuận tiện.

Chỉ có một điểm, đó là lỗ thoát nước của bồn tắm không được dọn dẹp, có lẽ là tóc của khách trước để lại, chỗ đó cảm thấy không thoải mái và thật đáng tiếc.

sample_comments.text:
Tôi đã ở lại qua đêm trước khi khởi hành vào buổi sáng từ sân bay Narita. 
Mặc dù việc làm thủ tục check-in diễn ra hơi muộn, nhưng sự phục vụ của nhân viên người nước ngoài rất nhanh nhẹn và tốt. 
Phòng ở có vẻ hơi cũ nhưng được bảo trì tốt và sạch sẽ. 
Wifi cũng không có vấn đề gì. 
Điểm đáng lưu ý là nhiệt độ điều hòa không khí không thể thay đổi ở mỗi phòng và chỉ có thể điều chỉnh bằng cách thay đổi lưu lượng gió nên hơi khó khăn. 
Vị trí thì tôi nghĩ là rất tốt. 
Ga JR Narita và ga Keisei Narita đều cách khoảng 2 phút đi bộ, xung quanh có nhiều nhà hàng và cửa hàng tiện lợi, nên tôi nghĩ không có gì phải lo lắng.

Also, the entire hotel is rather old and tired and could definitely use a renovation. The breakfast buffet was also not very tasty.The website mentions a gym, but staff told us that it hasn't been available since COVID.

sample_comments.title: This hotel is wretched!
sample_comments.positives: Beds were comfortable.
sample_comments.negatives: This place is a DUMP! We were shocked when we got into our room. Floors are absolutely disgusting, stains all over. The bathroom was moldy, caulk around the sink, shower walls, even the door had black surface mold growing. Pretty much every crevice in the bathroom had mold. There was no caulk around the tub and about a 6 MM gap, I don't want to imagine what was underneath. The smell was also off-putting, main room and bathroom both stank. I immediately went to the lobby and asked if this was normal, they gave us another room that really wasn't much better. Maybe a few less stains on the carpet and less smell in the bathroom of pee. The walls throughout the hotel were stained and wallpaper ripped. Base molding was filthy wherever you looked. The only positive was it was only one night.

sample_comments.room_type: Triple room, No view, Non-smoking (Triple Room [Non-Smoking] ☆ Free High-Speed Wi-Fi)

sample_comments.title: Acceptable
sample_comments.positives: The staff were professional, always available, and friendly/helpful when needed. The location is great, so close to the Narita JR and Keisei stations with many restaurants and convenience stores nearby.
sample_comments.negatives: The temperature in the guest rooms are controllable. We were informed the 'off' 'L' 'M' and 'H' buttons were all to control how much air enters the room, but we had no control over the temperature itself. In the winter all rooms get warm air and so the room was stuffy and too warm for us to sleep well. We tried opening the window but it doesn't stay open due to the weight of the curtains AND the train station is directly below the window so we were woken by platform announcements, whistles, and chimes all night/early morning. We didn't get good sleep at all for all (3) nights we spent there.

sample_comments.title: Very Disappointing Stay
sample_comments.text:
The room was quite old, dirty, and had an unpleasant smell. As soon as I opened the door, I noticed a strong cigarette odor, even though I had booked a non-smoking room.
The bathroom facilities were very outdated and difficult to use. The toilet did not flush properly and there was a strong musty smell. I also found hair on the bathroom floor, which was very unhygienic.
The hand soap bottle was broken and could not be pressed, and the soap inside was less than half full. Overall, the bathroom looked poorly maintained and gave a very bad impression.
The bed was extremely old, the mattress was worn out and uncomfortable, and the pillow was too small to sleep on properly.
Overall, this stay was very disappointing and did not meet basic expectations for cleanliness and comfort.

sample_comments.text: Very strong smell of stale smoke in all areas of hotel. No air con in the room and window locked. Hotel very worn with stained carpets and worn fixtures and fittings. I'd have left and booked somewhere else but I was exhausted after a long flight.

sample_comments.title: Creepy and deserted.
sample_comments.positives: The rooms were big
sample_comments.negatives: The hotel is very outdated. There are not many guests so there is this creepy haunting feeling throughout the hotel especially the hallways and elevators. At night time, the lights are dim and they play the creepy violin music around the elevator. The elevator is located next to the conference meeting hall. At night, the lights at the Conference Hall are off so you cannot see more than 6ft into the darkness. That coupled with the creepy violin music is like a scene right out of a horror movie.

sample_comments.title: Room wasn't clean and smelled weird

sample_comments.negatives: This hotel is very.. unsettling. It feels like its a liminal space found in a horror movie. There is no A/C and the beds are incredibly firm (and this is AFTER my whole japan trip). The shower pressure was painful, so I had to let it dribble water instead.

sample_comments.title: Buffet breakfast is bad. Staff not helpful. Room is v v small.
sample_comments.text: Not recommended. And v nosy during night because of the trains.

sample_comments.title: Very disappointed and surprised how dirty this hotel was.
sample_comments.positives: This hotel is located close to Keisei Narita and JR train station. Very convenient to restaurants and old Narita town is within walking distance.
sample_comments.negatives: This hotel is very old. We were assigned a hotle room which we had to cross over to another building. The rooms where very old and dirty The rooms are not very clean and the bathroom looked like no one had cleaned it for a while. I brought Clorox wipes and wiped down my bathroom as best I could. There was black mold on the ceiling, walls and the toilet. The hallway and rooms had a musty smell. The hallways smelled of cigarettes.

sample_comments.title: A Little TLC Would Help
sample_comments.text:
Stayed for one night at this hotel. The pros - a great location just a short train or subway ride from the airport. A nice little neighborhood with plenty of restaurant options. A good sized room.
The negatives. The hotel is in need of an update. Carpets are worn and not very clean. Really the least nice hotel room I stayed in during two weeks of travel in Japan. There is a health or fitness club that they charge extra for - so I cannot comment on it. A bit of a sad and neglected place which could offer a really nice stay if some money was invested in it. I would suggest you look at options in same location.

sample_comments.title: Above average
sample_comments.text: The hotel too old and dirty

sample_comments.title: Not what we expected
sample_comments.text: The location is not bad, and the parking is free, but the room isn't really clean, because there is a weird smell, and there are stains on the wall. The breakfast also isn't great, as there wasn't much to choose from, and it was the same food for 3 consecutive days.

sample_comments.title: Dirty hotel
sample_comments.text: Other than the good location, the hotel is maintained very poorly. So glad I was only staying one night, and couldn't wait to leave. My worst hotel experience in Japan.

sample_comments.title: Shocking Japan Standards
sample_comments.text: We checked in late at 2300H. I was shocked when I went into the bathroom. It smells really bad with urine stains all over the toilet bowl and floor. I had to use hot water to rinse it off. It was late and we were too tired to move to another room. It was raining and there were no air con but hot air coming from the central air con. Only selection of Low Med and High. Window can be opened but the there were constant grinding noise coming from outside. We didn't rest well at all. That will be my last stay at that hotel.

sample_comments.title: Old tired dirty rooms from 70s
sample_comments.text:
Close to train station and that is the only good thing about it. I booked a twin room but i didnt know only twin room is available in the old run down annex building from the 70s. Esculator at front entrance was broken. One out of three elevators were broken for a long time. Gym closed since Covid and never reopen. 

Room is big but old and run down. We changed rooms three times. First room had a mouldy smell and is next to railtrack. Very noisy and cannot sleep n relax when train go pass every 3 minutes. Second room had faulty main lights and engineer was not on site to fix it. Third room had a curry smell but i was too tired to move to a fourth room .

Staff helpfulness is a hit and miss. Female staff is generally friendly and helpful but male staff such as Mr 川原 (Kawahara?) just refused to do his job and provide any service, such as From answering queries to taking luggage to temp storage at all. I had to literally FORCE him to issue luggage tag and take in the luggage while he pretend to be busy counting blank paper from his reception drawer while avoiding eye contact.

sample_comments.title: No longer worth it
sample_comments.text: It's become a lot more expensive since 2019, and so for more money you get a much smaller room. The room was scruffy at best, very warm with a half-working aircon that took literally hours to get the room down to a passable temperature. The aircon is built into the bedding furniture, and it was absolutely disgusting. I can't believe that a machine so disgusting is allowed to blow any air into the room without health inspection closing the whole annex wing (which is where I was). Other than this, it was an okay business hotel.

sample_comments.title: Worse hotel ever stay in japan
sample_comments.text: Location is really good if you taking early morning flight and arrive late at night. HOWEVER, this hotel is really outdated! The room we stay is not clear at all a lot of stuffs in the room has old stain on it and it was rusted. Therefore i do not recommend this hotel unless you exhausted your other choices c/z tokyo hotels are expensive during weekend

sample_comments.title: Will not recommend

Pros: great location 
Cons: old facilities with no charge port near the bed. Quite dirty room, with obvious dirt in the fridge and bathroom. Heavy smell as well. 
Overall will not recommend

sample_comments.title: Air conditioning stinks. But otherwise ok
sample_comments.positives: Good location, nice hotel, staff were good
sample_comments.negatives: All guests complained about hot rooms. Air con not blowing cold had everyone hot. I complained and they tried to give me another room that was exactly the same situation and smelled like cigarettes so i declined. Got a fan from the staff. Windows do open on 11th floor so i could get some air, thank God. Outlets not convenient and no usb ports. Otherwise it was tolerable but i won't stay there again.

sample_comments.title: I left right away
sample_comments.text: The place does not really look like the pictures. The Place is so old and smells like moldy carpet. The beds were springy and uncomfortable with only 1 tiny pillow. I didn't bother staying. I just left and stayed in another hotel to avoid the headache. The only good thing about this place is the location. Its very near the train station and walking distance to places to see. I wish they would revamp and renovate the whole place. It's such a shame.

sample_comments.title: Easy access but old building
sample_comments.positives:
Easy access to stations and restaurants and drug store.
Rooms were fairly large for Japanese hotel. I believe our room had 22meter squared.
sample_comments.negatives: The building is outdated like a decade ago. Not enough electrical outlets and no electrical outlet by nightstand. Air conditioners were so outdated that it only has high, medium, low, off. No easily visible clock, so we had to keep looking at my wrist watch. Hotel has 2 buildings, and to access the "newer" building, you have to enter through main building, go up to 3rd floor and cross the walkway and access another elevator to your room. It's good if you're just staying the night to and straight to sleep, but I would put it lower priority on the list if you have other accommodation in mind.

sample_comments.title: Only thing Great is location
sample_comments.text: It is not up to the quality as it was named "Mercure narita hotel" . The room was old and need to update. We booked 2 nights but need to update the key card on the second day because it wont open the door. It was quite annoying to have to take 2 elevator and walk down to the counter to update the card. There is a mini fridge but no water bottle in the room. Guest need to take it by themselves, but nobody were giving this info. We book non-smoking room and we got no- smoking room but we can smell the cigarettes smoke in the hallway to the room. The only great thing about this hotel is the location that is right in middle of the JR narita station and Keisei Narita station.

sample_comments.negatives: room was old, bathroom had a lot of age, room was super super hot only way to get it to cool was open window all the way

sample_comments.title: There should have better choices
sample_comments.text: Although I am a frequent traveler to Japan, this was the first time stayed in this hotel. Compare to others, the price with the location like this is definitely the best as it is close to train station and bus terminal, if you don't mind the hotel just located right next to the railway track. But the room quality can ruin all your good impression. I am not saying the cleanliness, the room is average clean, we stayed in the extended building not the main hotel's building, the air-conditioning was awful, the controller only got 3 levels to adjust (low, mid, high), even you turned to the level high, the room still feel hot and humid, the service was good, after reporting the room status, they gave us an electric fan to cool down the room temperature. If you do not mind the room is a bit suffocating and you only stay for a night and departing from Japan the next day, this hotel could be on your list.

sample_comments.title: While the location is a major plus, the age of the facility may not meet everyone's expectations.

sample_comments.negatives: I found that the facilities could use some updates. The overall appearance feels a bit dated

sample_comments.title: Old hotel
sample_comments.text:
Honestly, skip this place if you're on vacation. The building is super old and my room didn't even have AC. The staff actually told me to just leave the window open to stay cool, but then I had to deal with the constant noise from the trains outside.

To top it off, there was a control panel in the middle of the room that literally said "Air Con," but it turned out to be a heater. Definitely not the vibe I wanted for my trip.

sample_comments.positives: recently stayed at Welco Hotel Narita and had a mixed experience. On the positive side, the staff were incredibly friendly and accommodating. The hotel is conveniently located near train stations and has a convenience store right in front, making it easy to grab essentials.
sample_comments.negatives: However, there were several drawbacks. The room was uncomfortably hot, and we requested two electric fans just to sleep comfortably. Additionally, we found the cleanliness of the rooms to be lacking. Our original room was dusty, and the bed sheets didn't seem fresh. The staff promptly transferred us to another room, but unfortunately, the new room also had a lot dust under the bed. The carpet was stained, and both the room and hallway had a smoke smell that was quite off-putting. At night, the hallways felt a bit eerie!

We were in just a standard room so i imagine that a higher floor or more deluxe room would be fine to stay.

sample_comments.title: The location is very good.
sample_comments.text:
The Keisei Narita Station and Narita Station are both very conveniently located within a 5-minute walk. There are convenience stores, drugstores, and restaurants nearby, so you won't have any trouble even if you stay for an extended period. Amenities can be requested at the front desk, and the rooms are spacious. I think this hotel is suitable for inbound travelers. 

The building shows signs of aging. In the room I stayed in, there were areas on the bathroom floor that sank when stepped on, which was a bit concerning. There are stains on the floors and curtains, so it may not be suitable for those seeking cleanliness. There were also other dirt spots in the bathroom aside from the wear and tear, which became noticeable since I stayed for a longer duration.

The staff members are very courteous. However, there seems to be a communication issue among the staff, as sometimes my requests were not accurately conveyed. 

During cleaning, they replaced my dishes, but for some reason, new dishes were placed on the desk and console table. I wondered if this was an effort to "place new dishes where the used ones were," but it made me anxious about whether they were truly new and they would collect dust. I believe it would be more pleasant if the replaced dishes were returned to their designated storage. 

Since I would like to use this hotel again, I wanted to express my thoughts honestly.

sample_comments.title: Convenient layover stay

This place is very convenient if you have early morning flight. The location is within 5 minutes of walking from the door step to JR or Keisei Train station. Also, they provide free airport transfer. So, if your flight is within peak period and you can't enjoy the free transfer, you can take the train which is just 8 minutes ride with less than 300¥. 

In terms of room, the room is a bit old. I stayed at 8th floor and you cannot adjust the air-conditioning as it is centralized. 

This hotel will definitely be my option again next time if my flight is within the peak period. 

Nevertheless, I enjoyed my stay due to the location which is right behind the train station.

sample_comments.title: Good hotel, one bad apple
sample_comments.text: I booked 2 rooms here for the night after we experienced an overnight delay at Narita Airport. We were exhausted after a whole day of traveling. I managed to find this place on Agoda and booked it very quickly as everything within airport radius was being sold out. Upon arriving, we were greeted by a female receptionist, who was professional and kind. While waiting to be checked in, a male receptionist with glasses was exhibiting very unprofessional behavior whenever his female colleague tried to assist us with our bags and some goodies. He motioned to her not to give us said goodies and to not help us with anything like our bags. I was traveling with my family which consisted of my wife, a senior, and a 2 year old baby. He did so in a way that was very much obvious as I was not the only one to observe it, as did my wife and another female employee in a backroom. To that gentleman, I will be writing a formal complaint to the respective parties which need to know about such conduct.

sample_comments.title: Japan
sample_comments.text: This hotel needs some work on it, but for the price it's clean and handy when getting off a plane tired. It has the convenience of having restaurants on the premises. It also has a free shuttle bus service to and from the airport. It's right on the edge of the old town of Narita, so great for getting out and doing a historical walk. It's across the road from the train station that goes directly to Tokyo or the airport. It's about an hour+ to Tokyo by train. Try not to have a room overlooking the train station as it's noisy in the early morning hours and hit from the morning sun, as their AC is useless and I baked in the room. Good value for the money if on a budget.

sample_comments.title: Good
sample_comments.text: For the first room checked in, water leaks found at the bottom next to toilet bowl. Requested for change of room. In the second room, molds were found on ceiling and joints silicone in the bathroom.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.text: Used to have free transport to airport during the Mercure management. After new management, it has been removed. Disappointing.

sample_comments.title: Disappointing stay
sample_comments.text:
We had stayed at this hotel when it was the Mercure and loved it. The hotel has deteriorated and is now very basic. Our beds were not made on second day, on shuttle bus service to the airport. Hotel looked tired carpets worn and repainting required.
It is in a great location if you want to visit Narita. Many terrific places to eat nearby. The hotel requires refurbishing 
To

sample_comments.title: It's a very old hotel
sample_comments.text: Good location, 2 minutes walk from metro station and 5 minutes to JR station. The receptionist are expats, so they can communicate well in English, it's a foreigner friendly hotel. The cleanliness is in doubt! It's the worst hotel i stayed in my two weeks trip. It's an old and poorly maintained hotel. They gave me a room with terrible smell (a mixture of mold and some other funny smell, yuk!). They let me stayed in another room after my request.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.text:
Couldn't turn off my air conditioning/ventilation which makes a significant sound and hindered my sleep.
At the same time, the air of the room was too stuffy.
The staff were all nice and helpful.
The location is great for people who want to be close to the airport

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.text: Convienient location old rooms

sample_comments.title: good place to stay before heading to Narita Airport
sample_comments.text: The hotel is located just a station away from Narita Airport. It is like an old deserted hotel in the lobby and passage way with relatively clean room and bathroom. It is very near to the station and it is also near to shuttle bus bringing you to Aeon Mall not to far away.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.text: Noisy because so close to the train station.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.text: Basic room. Needs some upgrading and if you are in the annex it is a bit annoying having to take two elevators

sample_comments.title: Only the location is good
sample_comments.text:
Location and the size is the only recommendation. Right in front train station and big size. Free parking 

Cons is: the hotel looks old. The toilet which we can't accept, dirt on the knot, rusty mirror. Yellowish bath tub. 
We went on last week of Nov, outside is cold but the room is hot which can't on aircon. The only way is open window which is too noisy until we can't sleep, the car and the Train keep pass by,

sample_comments.title: Good stay next to Narita Station
sample_comments.text: Stay was near airport. Room was nice. Staff was indifferent.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.text: The corridoe at the Annex building smells.
sample_comments.room_type: Standard Twin Room With Extra Bed-Non-Smoking

sample_comments.title: Near to the shopping belt
sample_comments.text: I walk from the hotel to Naritsan temple. It is just about 10 mins away by walking. The airport is also very near via train or taxi.

sample_comments.title: Main tower better than the annex
sample_comments.text: I stayed for 2 nights. First in the main tower and second night in the annex. I noticed that the rooms in annex were significantly older and worse condition, especially the bathroom could be refitted. I think price was the same, so it's random what you get. Breakfast is too basic for the price, so I would just use convenience store instead. Anyway rooms were a bit old but clean enough and the location couldn't be better, so it's ok for the price.

sample_comments.title: Very Convenient location
sample_comments.text: A 3 mins walk from two train stations with direct access to Narita plus the benefits of food and sights thrown in. Aeon and DonQ is also accessible via bus. Negative was an older and less clean room, a common encounter when using airport hotels.

sample_comments.title: Perfect for a Short Stay
sample_comments.text: Hotel Welco is conveniently close to the airport, and we stayed here for a couple of days as a rest stop before boarding a long flight from Japan to the US. The best thing about this hotel is its location--it is within walking distance to a train station, and there are many restaurants and convenience stores close by. The rooms themselves are comfortable enough, but mediocre at best. The hotel is a bit rundown, and has certainly seen better days. However, for the price we paid, we really couldn't expect more. It is the location that makes Hotel Welco worth the price.

sample_comments.title: 場所はいいですが、設備が古い
sample_comments.text:
出張で泊まらせていただきました。
スタッフの方は親切で、アクセスは非常によかったのですが、設備が古く、窓から、隙間を通る音が聞こえたので、ちょっと残念でした。
パッキンが悪いようなので、補修をされると良いと思います。

sample_comments.title: Hotel review - Decent
sample_comments.text:
Decent hotel experience.
Old hotel but fairly clean.
Biggest con is that it is right next to the train station.
Take note that there is no free shuttle service to Narita airport

sample_comments.title: Easy access to airport
sample_comments.text: Good hotel to stay for easy access to Narita. Some great food option close by.

sample_comments.title: Dated
sample_comments.text: Old faculty, close to train stations, very noisy.

sample_comments.title: Really Good.
sample_comments.text: Great location, can be loud because of 2 train lines but can't really complain. Very convenient.

sample_comments.title: Old, smoke smell
sample_comments.text: The room was old with smoke smell, bed was not comfortable, pillow was good though

sample_comments.title: Near Keisei Narita Station
sample_comments.text: Big room around 28sqm but very old.

sample_comments.title: Welco
sample_comments.text:
Great location, great staff.
Rooms getting a little dated

sample_comments.title: For ¥7000 good deal
sample_comments.text:
I got a great deal on this room. The room was spacious bigger than most hotel rooms. However, the bathroom was a bit dated and smelly- a combination of toilet odor and the musty, old shower curtain. 

It was nice to be able to open the window but noise from the train station was a bit annoying so couldn't be kept open. 

The validated free parking was very appreciated.

I would stay here again if I got the same price but I wouldn't if the price was over ¥8500. 

Overall pretty good though and the location was an interesting area with a lot of restaurants and convenient.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.text:
The location of this hotel is amazing but the hotel is dated and is need of a serious uplift 
the corridor of the rooms needs to be well lite and also it's giving me The Shinning vibe the corridor echo especially in the Annex wing 
I would really hope they quickly do an uplift as the location of the hotel is superb. My view is if you'll can beat with the dates 80s style hotel then stay here else I won't suggest as there are a few more modern hotels around this area

sample_comments.title: Big room budget hotel
sample_comments.text: Provide free parking .Annex building can hear running train but it's ok.Bathroom had some smell but staff cleaned again quickly by ozone machine,thanks.

sample_comments.title: Good location,economic
sample_comments.text: Great spot next to station and village,has all you need and no frills,

sample_comments.title: Good enough
sample_comments.text: Very average stay, but a very good location.

sample_comments.title: Good location
sample_comments.text:
Pros: nice location. Between Narita Station and Keisei-Station. Directly access to the old Narita, to all the shops and restaurants. Ok for 1 or 2 nights before taking a flight.
The staff are helpful and speak English. 

Cons: if you're a light sleeper, you can hear the trains passing by or the clients of the hotel talking in the hallway. That's because of the metal doors which doesn't block noises. The it's an old hotel and would seriously need an overhaul.

sample_comments.title: FREE BUS TO AIRPORT
sample_comments.text: The room is big compared to other hotels we stayed in Tokyo. They provide free transport to Narita Airport. Good for those who wants to catch an early flight. Earliest bus was at 7:00AM and we reached airport about 20 mins before 8:00AM.

sample_comments.title: Convenient and affordable
sample_comments.positives: Amazing location, extremely friendly stuff. If you like to sleep under the cold A/C that would not be a good choice. Plus place need some room renovations BUT for the price and location that's the best option. Convenient store, pharmacy, cafes, train station - everything is steps away. I would recommend this place!
sample_comments.negatives: A/c not cooling properly

sample_comments.title: Great location
sample_comments.text: Hotel was walking distance from 2 train stations. Lots of convenience stores around. Nice takoyaki outside the higashi train station which we could get on the way back to hotel. Walking distance to the shrine where lots of eateries along the way. Grilled eel rice, ramen, tempura. Pancakes hot and cold with various types of fillings inside for afternoon snacks or brekkie available along way to shrine. Free shuttle to airport but traffic can be unexpected, train is a better option. Got a twin room and this allowed us to rest better each nite. Room was relatively big for Tokyo standard. Quite a nice stay.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.negatives: I've stayed at Welco frequently for work and leisure throughout the past 2 years. This particular visit, the staff were less helpful and hospitable than in previous visits.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.text: The facility of the hotel needs to be upgraded

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Location was great. Although rooms need better upkeep.
sample_comments.negatives: Rooms A bit dirty and old

sample_comments.title: Great location, decent sized rooms
sample_comments.text: Great location, just at the corner of the airport line and JR line station. Bus stops are close by. Good place to explore Narita. The room is decent sized, a bit dated with the furniture/interior.

sample_comments.title: Can't beat the price!
sample_comments.positives: The location
sample_comments.negatives: It was old. The single pane window was noisy.

sample_comments.title: Ok
sample_comments.text:
Very dated. No temperature control for air con. Toilet was running, room very dated. Bed with springs, some of which were very noticeable. 
Front desk staff very nice, housekeeping passed with no acknowledgement. Bathroom smelled like mildew. 
Great location situated between JR and Kesei lines. 
Fast internet. Clean linens. Laundry facility. 
Good budget hotel in a great location. Shop around.

sample_comments.title: Very good
sample_comments.positives: Breakfast was good.
sample_comments.negatives:
The room was so warm that we had to open the window. 
The beds were very hard. The pillow was hard and too small.

sample_comments.title: complicated
